name: Moving Platform
description: Moving Platform is a world-system mechanic driven by forces, constraints, collision response, or simulated motion.
design_notes: Moving Platform should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For physics mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use moving platform as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect moving platform to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels the world has weight, motion, and consequences.

parameters.name: force_strength
parameters.description: Magnitude of the force, impulse, or constraint effect.

parameters.name: mass_or_weight
parameters.description: Mass scale used by collision and force calculations.

parameters.name: damping
parameters.description: How quickly motion loses energy over time.

edge_cases: Moving Platform is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Moving Platform overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Moving Platform runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Moving Platform state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Moving Platform plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Moving Platform produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Moving Platform strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Moving Platform becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Moving Platform with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Moving Platform as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Moving Platform with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Moving Platform as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
